mnemonics: A Regular chain is STABLE: Steady State, Time-Averaged, Balancing, Long-term Equilibrium.
keyPoints: A stationary distribution π satisfies π = πP, where P is the transition matrix.
A Markov chain is regular if some power of its transition matrix has all positive entries.
Absorbing states are traps; once entered, they cannot be left. Their probability in the stationary distribution is 1.
commonMistakes: Assuming all Markov chains have a unique stationary distribution (only regular ones do).
Confusing stationary distribution with limiting distribution; they are the same for regular chains.
